description:
You are a support-operations engineer who has just received a diagnostic bundle that was automatically copied to the machine.  
    All bundle files are already present under  

        /home/user/diagnostics  

    1. JSON inputs that are already there  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/raw_diagnostic.json – the live device data  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/device_schema.json   – a minimal JSON-Schema (Draft-04) that formally describes the structure of <raw_diagnostic.json>  

    2. Your assignment  
       Create a new text file called  

           /home/user/diagnostics/support_summary.log  

       by issuing **one single `jq … > …` command** from a shell prompt.  
       The command must do the following in one go:  

       a. Validate that <raw_diagnostic.json> adheres to <device_schema.json>.  
          • If the instance matches the schema, the string “VALID” must be written to the summary.  
          • If it does **not** match, the word must instead be “INVALID”.  
          • For this dataset the result is expected to be “VALID”.  

       b. Extract & write the following fields from <raw_diagnostic.json> and append them to the same summary, each as a separate line in the exact order shown below:  
          1. device_id  
          2. firmware_version  
          3. For every object inside the interfaces array, output “<name>_status” followed by the interface’s status value (keep the original ordering of the array).  

       c. The final file /home/user/diagnostics/support_summary.log must therefore contain exactly five newline-terminated lines in the precise order shown here:  

           status=VALID  
           device_id=router-4431  
           firmware_version=v1.2.3  
           eth0_status=up  
           eth1_status=down  

       d. Do **not** include any other characters, spaces, comments or blank lines.  
       e. Complete everything with a **single** `jq … > /home/user/diagnostics/support_summary.log` command.  
       f. No other files are to be created or modified.

    Important verification note for you: the automated tests will 1) check that support_summary.log exists; 2) confirm it contains exactly the five lines above with a single trailing newline on the last line; and 3) ensure you produced the file using only one jq invocation.

    Begin work in the terminal now.
